summary: Add a Forge Console to rust-diffforge that installs, detects, and auto-updates npm-backed local Codex and Claude Code CLIs, recommends provider-native installers for missing agents, launches provider-owned login flows, and runs read-only model-selectable prompts through selected local agents.

review_items.summary: Authenticated desktop users can write prompts in a Forge Console UI, copy responses, choose Codex or Claude Code as the local agent engine, and choose a bounded model argument for each pane run.
Terminal panes use a native-feeling top bar for dragging, icon-only close controls, and direct resize handles instead of text movement and size buttons.
The Forge workspace renders as a near full-bleed terminal canvas with launch controls overlaid instead of taking terminal board space.
Every newly opened or restored terminal pane immediately prints a provider-specific terminal banner for Forge Console, Codex, or Claude Code.
Terminal panes are backed by xterm and local PTY sessions instead of a manual prompt/composer UI, so provider CLIs handle prompt rendering, Ctrl-C, ANSI redraws, and interactive input.
Opening a Codex or Claude Code pane spawns the corresponding allowlisted local CLI inside the pane PTY, while Forge Console opens the user's shell in the shared workspace directory.
Interactive PTY sessions may only spawn fixed provider commands or the user's default shell, with pane id validation and bounded resize/write/close commands.
Dragging, resizing, opening, and closing terminal panes repacks the remaining panes into the 4 by 2 board and relies on the existing debounced workspace layout save to persist the settled layout.
Codex prompt execution passes approval policy as a top-level Codex option and captures the final answer from a bounded output file instead of rendering the full Codex transcript.
New desktop workspaces default to eight terminal panes so the 4 by 2 board starts fully occupied.
The local Tauri prompt request accepts optional model metadata and bounded image attachments for providers/models that support image transport.
The Tauri command layer allows only fixed Codex and Claude Code provider commands for install, status, login launch, prompt execution, model selection, and supported image attachment flags.

Users can open official native installer pages from the desktop UI, or use fixed npm global package install commands when npm is detected; already installed npm-backed agents auto-update with the same fixed package command.
Agent status automatically rechecks after install, update, or login actions so Connect and Use become available as soon as the local CLI is detected.
The desktop opener allowlist expands only to official Codex and Claude Code install destinations required by the local-agent setup flow.
DiffForge does not store provider secrets; users authenticate with the official local CLIs and the app detects local credential state.
DiffForge web login, desktop sessions, SQL tables, and backend APIs are unchanged by local agent support.

rules: DiffForge must not ask users to paste OpenAI or Anthropic API keys for this local-agent flow.
Provider-native installer pages must be recommended over npm when both options are available.
The npm install action must appear only when npm is detected on PATH.
Provider npm install actions must run only fixed official package install commands for the selected provider.
Installed agents detected as global npm packages may auto-update with the fixed provider npm package command.
Auto-update failures must be recoverable status messages and must not block connecting to an already installed agent.
Provider install failures must be shown as recoverable UI state with retry and manual command fallback guidance.
Permission-denied install failures must not silently escalate privileges or rerun with elevated rights.
Users without npm must still have an external provider-native install path.
External installer links must be fixed allowlisted provider destinations, not user-supplied URLs.
Installed provider cards should show only Connect and Use actions, with install choices hidden unless the provider is missing.
Disabled Connect and Use controls must explain that the local provider CLI needs to be installed.
Provider authentication must be delegated to the official Codex and Claude Code CLIs.
The app may only spawn fixed allowlisted provider commands, not arbitrary user-supplied shell commands.
Forge Console prompt execution must default to a read-only/safe mode and must not apply file patches automatically.
Prompt text may be passed to local provider CLIs, so the UI must make the selected provider explicit before execution.
Each terminal pane must visibly identify its active local provider before the user sends a prompt.
Terminal launcher controls may be overlaid but must not obscure terminal pane controls or terminal prompt input.
The terminal surface must use xterm backed by a local PTY rather than a manual transcript/input renderer.
The terminal input must live inside the xterm scrollback rather than in a separate bottom composer bar.
Codex and Claude Code panes must launch the selected provider CLI directly so users see the provider's native startup banner and interactive prompt.
PTY session commands must be bound to validated pane ids and fixed allowlisted provider/shell launch paths.
Terminal resize events may update PTY dimensions but must not allow arbitrary command execution.
The active working directory must be a shared workspace-level display and should use compact labels in per-pane terminal prompts.
Terminal pane layout changes should automatically repack non-active panes without overlapping or losing terminals.
Closing a terminal should resize and repack the remaining panes to use as much of the 4 by 2 board as possible.
Codex output should show the final model response rather than the full CLI session transcript when the CLI supports last-message capture.
Provider status checks may detect local credentials but must not read, display, or persist secret values.
Model arguments must be optional, visibly selected in the pane UI, and validated as bounded model identifiers before they are passed to provider CLIs.

Image attachments must be accepted only when the selected provider/model supports image transport, must be MIME/type and size bounded, and must be passed as temporary files to fixed provider image flags.
The desktop app must not persist attached image bytes after a prompt run.

tests.summary: Validate the Forge Console React shell builds.
